arguments.task: Open official Salesforce developer and pricing pages. Determine: one-line product/API description, all documented API auth methods, API surface types (REST, GraphQL, or both) and breadth, whether Salesforce has an official MCP server/toolkit page, access tier and any self-serve or paid gating from official pricing/developer pages, rate limit notes, and buildability for an AI-agent toolkit. Only use official Salesforce pages that load in browser and capture their canonical URLs and supporting text.

input.thought: Start browser research on official Salesforce sources for docs, auth, API, pricing, and MCP evidence.

raw_result.description: CRM platform with APIs for accessing Salesforce data, metadata, and platform services from external applications.
raw_result.auth_notes: Salesforce REST API documentation explicitly points developers to use OAuth 2.0 with connected apps/external client apps for authorization. No additional first-party evidence was gathered in this pass for API keys, Basic auth, or personal access tokens, so they are not included.
raw_result.gating_reason: Official Salesforce pricing and GraphQL documentation indicate API access/features depend on Salesforce editions, with GraphQL API called out for Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer editions rather than universal free self-serve access.

api_surface.breadth: Salesforce exposes multiple API families including REST and GraphQL, with broad platform coverage for records/data and related platform capabilities; browser-gathered evidence also referenced other API families such as SOAP.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No official Salesforce MCP server or Model Context Protocol toolkit page was verified from first-party evidence gathered in this pass.

raw_result.blocker: Public developer docs and OAuth-based API documentation are available, but practical toolkit coverage depends on having a suitable Salesforce edition/org with API access rather than universally available free self-serve access.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Browser-gathered evidence from the Salesforce platform API limits cheat sheet indicated core daily API request allocations such as 15,000 calls for Developer Edition and 100,000+ base for Enterprise editions; limits vary by edition/org.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer docs are extensive, covering REST and GraphQL APIs plus platform API limits. GraphQL availability is edition-specific, which is an important packaging signal for agent toolkit support.
raw_result.human_review_reason: The structured field mcp_exists is not strongly supported by first-party evidence gathered here because absence was inferred from unsuccessful official-page discovery rather than a verified official statement. Access-tier nuance may also vary by edition/org, but the primary review trigger is MCP existence evidence.
